title: Executable-owned versioned skill bundles
description: Keep deployed agent guidance synchronized with the real command contract.
decision: Embed Codex and Claude Code Ledger skill bundles and a manifest in the executable distribution. The manifest declares executable version range, CLI contract versions, provider, bundle checksum, and operation IDs. tally skill list and check are read-only; tally skill install is idempotent, refuses incompatible bundles before writes, installs only through provider-specific project/user locations, and teaches public commands rather than database access.
rationale: The executable and operation registry are the only reliable compatibility authorities. This mirrors Lex distribution while avoiding separately versioned prose that can teach obsolete commands.
alternatives: A: repo-only skills — rejected for fresh installs. B: remotely fetched latest skills — rejected for offline and version drift. C: embedded version-matched bundles — chosen.
consequences: Skill source and embedded copies require parity tests. Installation mutates host files and follows the same artifact idempotency protocol. No skill can bypass the CLI contract or access SQLite directly.
status_notes: Accepted. Planned implementation areas: src/Tally/Skills and packaged provider bundles.
